title: SARS-CoV-2 Antibody Dynamics in Immunocompromised and Healthy Individuals
authors_full: Gabrielle Rogers¹, Shivani Shah¹, and Yunro Chung¹–³
authors_short: Gabrielle Rogers Shivani Shah & Yunro Chung

summary.hook: This poster analyzes large-scale longitudinal serology data (1,767 participants; 4,582 samples) to characterize how SARS-CoV-2 antibody responses change over time in...
summary.summary_30s: This poster analyzes large-scale longitudinal serology data (1,767 participants; 4,582 samples) to characterize how SARS-CoV-2 antibody responses change over time in immunocompromised groups (autoimmune disease, cancer, HIV, solid organ transplant recipients) versus healthy controls.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster analyzes large-scale longitudinal serology data (1,767 participants; 4,582 samples) to characterize how SARS-CoV-2 antibody responses change over time in...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Observational longitudinal serology analysis with statistical modeling (nonlinear mixed-effects).. 1,767 participants; 4,582 longitudinal samples
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Dataset size: 1,767 participants contributed 4,582 longitudinal samples across four institutions.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Accurate interpretation of seroprevalence and immunity durability requires modeling heterogeneous antibody kinetics across clinical cohorts.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: Which SARS-CoV-2 antigens and antibody isotypes are included in the plotted “antibody response,” and what units/transformations are used?

key_numbers: Overall trend panel spans approximately April 2021–September 2022; booster trend panel spans approximately June...
Nonlinear mixed-effects models found significant group × spline time interaction (p<0.05).
Vaccination × infection status interaction was significant (p<0.05).
takeaways: Dataset size: 1,767 participants contributed 4,582 longitudinal samples across four institutions.
Study cohorts: autoimmune disease, cancer, HIV, solid organ transplant recipients, and healthy controls.
Authors’ stated conclusions: Not stated as a “Conclusion” section; the background and modeling notes imply that antibody trajectories are heterogeneous and that cohort...
